seoTitle: How to Turn On Spell Check in Outlook Easily (2026)
metaDescription: Catch typos before they go out by enabling automatic spell check and grammar review in Outlook, including real-time underlines as you type.
intro: A typo in a client email can undercut an otherwise professional message. Outlook has proofing settings that catch spelling and grammar mistakes automatically, both before you hit send and in real time as you type, so you can rely on the software to flag problems before they leave your outbox.

steps.heading: Open File Options
steps.body: Go to Outlook's File menu and open the Options dashboard.

steps.heading: Enable check spelling before sending
steps.body: In the Mail category, check the box for always checking spelling before sending so nothing goes out with an obvious mistake.

steps.heading: Explore spelling and autocorrect settings
steps.body: Click into the Spelling and Autocorrect sub-menu to see the full range of proofing options available.

steps.heading: Turn on real-time underlines
steps.body: Toggle on the setting to check spelling as you type, which underlines potential mistakes while you're still composing.

steps.heading: Adjust grammar and style strictness
steps.body: Open the Editor settings to fine-tune how strict the grammar and style checking should be, then save and test with a draft message.

faq.question: Does Outlook check spelling automatically by default?
faq.answer: Not always with every setting enabled; you may need to manually turn on options like checking spelling before sending in the File Options menu.

faq.question: Can I get grammar suggestions, not just spelling?
faq.answer: Yes, the Editor settings let you adjust checking strictness to include style and grammar reviews beyond basic spelling.

faq.question: How do I reset words I've told Outlook to ignore?
faq.answer: You can clear your custom ignored words dictionary from the spelling settings so previously skipped words get flagged again.

faq.question: Will spell check slow down my typing?
faq.answer: Real-time underlining runs in the background and shouldn't noticeably affect typing speed, but you can turn it off if you find it distracting.
